mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising or sits above the prior 6-month average, markets price a tighter policy path and the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days as term premium and expected short rates reprice upward.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is flat or falling yet the 10-year yield still rises in the majority of 20-day windows, or if the sign of the 20-day yield change is negative in most periods when FEDFUNDS trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE by at least 0.2 percentage points over a short window signals weakening labor demand and slower growth expectations, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield lower within the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE threshold crossings are followed by positive 20-day Treasury yield changes more than 60% of the time, or if the average 20-day yield change after the trigger is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broad demand strength in employment and consumer spending, which lifts growth and inflation expectations and drives the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone, or if 20-day Treasury yield changes are not positive in a majority of joint-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains weak, industrial activity outpaces housing supply expansion and the market reads the combination as a cyclical upswing, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises and HOUST stays below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not improve predictive power relative to INDPRO alone, or if the 20-day yield change is not positive after the trigger in a statistically meaningful majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.5% creates a policy-lag signal: inflation pressure rises faster than policy rates, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises as inflation compensation and tightening expectations reprice.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation threshold episodes are followed by flat or falling 10-year yields, or if the joint CPI-FEDFUNDS condition has no better hit rate than CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained advance in GDPC1 over the prior quarter implies stronger real activity and higher expected nominal growth, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDP growth is not followed by higher 10-year yields over the next 60 trading days in most instances, or if the average forward yield change is negative after GDP expansions.

mechanism_narrative: In a weak labor market regime, falling PAYEMS together with rising UNRATE signals a deteriorating employment backdrop that lowers expected growth and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration signal is followed by positive 20-day yield changes more than 40% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power over either labor series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recovery phase, a rebound in HOUST above its recent average while RSAFS remains firm signals broad cyclical demand momentum, and the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations strengthen.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-retail co-strength does not precede higher 10-year yields over 60 trading days, or if the signal fails specifically in post-recovery periods where demand broadening should be most visible.
